title: FIN-Seq (Frozen Immunolabeled Nuclei Sequencing)
author: Ryoji Amamoto
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zxbf7in
materials: Prepare all solutions and keep on ice with the Dounce homogenizer.

From the Recoverall RNA/DNA Isolation Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific AM1975), mix 100 uL of Digestion Buffer and 4 uL of protease for each sample. Adjust accordingly based on the volume of the leftover supernatant.

steps.title: Fill the glass Dounce homogenizer with...
steps.instruction: Fill the glass Dounce homogenizer with 1 mL of cold homogenization buffer.

steps.title: Mince the tissue into little pieces...
steps.instruction: Mince the tissue into little pieces and place in 1% PFA for 5 minutes on ice.

steps.title: Transfer the tissue pieces into the homogenizer
steps.instruction: Transfer the tissue pieces into the homogenizer. With the tight pestle, homogenize with 10-15 strokes on ice. Avoid foaming.

steps.title: Transfer the homogenate into a 5...
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenate into a 5 mL polypropylene tube (Thermo Fisher 14-959-11A).

steps.title: Carefully add 2 mL of Sucrose...
steps.instruction: Carefully add 2 mL of Sucrose Bed solution to the bottom of the tube so that the homogenate is above the sucrose solution.

steps.title: Spin at 500xg, 12 minutes, 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Spin at 500xg, 12 minutes, 4 degrees.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant. Add 1 mL of 4% PFA solution and resuspend pellet. Incubate for 15 minutes at 4 degrees with rocking.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer. Incubate for 15 minutes at 4 degrees with rocking.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees. Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with Primary Antibody in Blocking Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with Primary Antibody in Blocking Buffer. Concentration depends on primary antibody. Generally, higher concentration than immunohistochemistry is necessary. Incubate for 30 minutes at 4 degrees with rocking.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees. Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer. Incubate for 5 minutes on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees. Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with appropriate Secondary Antibody (1:1000) in Blocking Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with appropriate Secondary Antibody (1:1000) in Blocking Buffer. Incubate at 4 degrees for 30 minutes with rocking.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer. Incubate for 5 minutes on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees. Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with Blocking Buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000xg for 5 minutes at 4 degrees.

steps.title: Gate based on Hoechst histogram, as shown below
steps.instruction: Gate based on Hoechst histogram, as shown below. This step will ensure that you get 2N nuclei. Make sure not to include 4N nuclei.

steps.title: Gate using the plot with appropriate wavelengths
steps.instruction: Gate using the plot with appropriate wavelengths. Often, the separation will not be as obvious as GFP or well characterized cell surface markers. Thus, it’s important to have proper controls.

steps.title: Filter and proceed to FACS
steps.instruction: Filter and proceed to FACS.

steps.title: The hoechst histogram on the left
steps.instruction: The hoechst histogram on the left. Make sure to gate the 2N nuclei. Looking at all of the 2N nuclei, look for separation of immunolabeled population (here, CAR antibody). For the first few times, it'll be important to have a secondary only control. Often, the separation of the immunolabeled population will not be as obvious. It may appear as a small bump.

steps.title: Spin at 3000xg for 7 minutes at 4 degrees
steps.instruction: Spin at 3000xg for 7 minutes at 4 degrees.

steps.title: Remove as much supernatant as possible
steps.instruction: Remove as much supernatant as possible.

steps.title: Incubate at 50 degrees for 3...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50 degrees for 3 hours (wrap the lid with parafilm). Note that this step differs from the manufacturer’s protocol.

steps.title: The samples can be stored at...
steps.instruction: The samples can be stored at -80 indefinitely after incubation or proceed to next steps according to the kit protocol.

steps.title: Elute in ~17 uL of UltraPure water
steps.instruction: Elute in ~17 uL of UltraPure water.

steps.title: Store RNA at -80 degrees
steps.instruction: Store RNA at -80 degrees.

steps.title: FACS isolated nuclei are sorted into...
steps.instruction: FACS isolated nuclei are sorted into Blocking Buffer and kept at 4 degrees.

steps.title: It’s possible to run a RNA...
steps.instruction: It’s possible to run a RNA pico chip on the BioAnalyzer (Agilent), but RIN will not be accurate because rRNA is not enriched in nuclei. Qubit or BioAnalyzer can be used to estimate RNA concentration. If less than 10,000 nuclei, concentration may not be available by these methods.

steps.title: Proceed to the SMART-Seq v
steps.instruction: Proceed to the SMART-Seq v.4 protocol for cDNA synthesis and amplification.

steps.title: If RNA concentration is too low...
steps.instruction: If RNA concentration is too low for Qubit or BioAnalyzer, >16 rounds of amplification may be necessary to generate enough cDNA. HS DNA chip should be run on the BioAnalyzer after the SMART-Seq v4 protocol and after Nextera indexing to ensure proper library construction.
